text: The surgeon uses monopolar diathermy to divide the falciform ligament, exposing the morgue agnese hernia on either side. The hernial defect is dissected, revealing the hernial content, including the transverse colon lying within the defect. The surface area is made raw to facilitate repair.

The surgeon meticulously dissects and removes excess fat that has migrated into the Morgagni's hernia. The superior and inferior margins of the hernia sac are clearly dissected, revealing the defect and hernia. The falciform ligament is divided to free the inferior surface of the diaphragm.

The surgeon uses non-absorbable barbed sutures to repair the hernia defect, starting from the right side and progressing to the left side. The sutures are pulled to obliterate the defect, creating a two-layer repair without the need for mesh. The suturing process is repeated from left to right to reinforce the repair.

The surgeon takes the last suture bite in a reverse manner to self-lock the suture without formal knotting. The suture is then divided with scissors, completing the repair. The cholecystectomy site is visible, indicating the end of the surgery.
